title: hieracium caesium subsp. orthocolon
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. orthocolon, also known as Kite's milkwort or mountain hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and feathery foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium caesium subsp. orthocolon include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
